text: The range of ways this headset can connect to your tv and devices was the main thing that attracted me to this model. Whether you want to connect to a TV, a receiver, a computer, a tablet or a phone, there is probably a way to do it. You can use Bluetooth, or you can go the wired route through the transmitter and included cables. The first method I tested was the transmitter connected to my computer using the 3.5 mm cable. As soon as I turned on both the headphones and the transmitter, they connected instantly without the need to pair them. That was a good start! I used YouTube on my laptop as my first test source and spent a bit of time cycling through the EQ settings by double-clicking the multifunction button. That toggles between (tv) voice/speech mode, standard, and bass heavy. For me, standard was easily the best. It struck the right balance - the bass was still present, the treble was still clear, and overall it felt natural. It is a challenge for some earbud designs since the cup holding the tip would need to be larger so the earbuds fit, but then the stem with the charging pads might wobble. text: the QuietComfort Ultra, which are way overpriced, and the main selling point (their spatial sound or whatever they call it) is a joke. I dont think it enhances the sound at all. In fact, music has a hollow quality when using it. Additionally, I prefer the QuietComforts buttons to the Ultras touch controls, which seemed awkward. Additionally, the Ultra utilize a 35mm driver, while the QuietComfort use a 40mm driver. While the Ultra may be more efficient, I think the low end frequencies suffer compared to the QuietComfort. The Bose QuietComfort are quickly becoming my favorite. The sound quality is second to none. It is noticeably better than the QC 45. The bass is definitely deeper and punchier. The highs and mids are also discernibly better. The highs are brighter and more crisp, though the highs are never harsh on my ears. The 45s require a lot of equalizer use (+6, 0, -3), while the QuietComfort still require some tinkering, they require a lot less of it to achieve optimum sound (+1, +2, +1). Even at -3 on the EQ for the highs on the 45s, the higher frequencies feel like they need to be reined in. Indeed, the QuietComfort just sound more refined. The noise cancelling has also improved, though I would say both the Sony XM5s and the Sonos Aces are a touch better at cancelling most noise. I run an air purifier at arms length away from my chair, and a window a/c to supplement the central air, which is directly behind my head. With the XM5s I can completely eliminate the noise from both while theyre simultaneously running. With the new QuietComfort I can just barely detect the a/c, while with the 45s I can easily pick up the a/c. The difference here between the Sonys and the new QuietComfort is marginal at best. I should mention that part of Boses way to cancel noise is to pump low-volume white noise into the headphones, which seems counterintuitive, but I dont mind it. Others may mind it, however. The QuietComfort are the most-comfortable to wear, though they are no different than the 45s in that regard. They are extremely lightweight (though sturdy), and clamp down perfectly tight. text: I am happily surprised by this set of headphones. The box was very nondescript, and I had thought it was a bit sketchy. My first impressions were the headphones were cheap. I was wrong, they fit very well and had great tone and bass. The sound quality is very good, I am using my most basic headphone cords and the sound quality is excellent. So don't let the price point hold you back; this is a very good value for a set of headphones. Now I will have to find out how long they will last. That will have to be another review. This product is a great alternative to ear buds!! The bass is incredible I just received these yesterday. They are comfortable and sound very good, especially for only $19.99, but certainly not worth the list price of $199.99. I imagine thats just marketing. When the noise cancelling is activated, I can hear a very faint ocean wave white noise sound, which I find annoying. I am very sound sensitive, and my house is usually very quiet, so I wont be using that feature. I imagine that it would not be noticeable if it were masking sustained background noise. They connected easily to my Echo dot, seem to be constructed well and should last quite a while. The range seems to be very good also. I can walk around my house with them on and remain connected.
product_name: Bluetooth Headphones with Active Noise Cancelling (ANC), Wireless Over Ear Bluetooth Headsets with 35H Playtime, Travel, Black

text: Love these headphones. I got them after loosing my AirPods & I needed something else fast for the gym. Great for the gym & it doesnt slip off my head, it stays in place when Im working out. The only thing is it doesnt have noise cancellation but I dont think for these headphones they claim to have it. The quality is great & its clear. It paired just fine with my iPhone. The battery life lasts a long time. The price is great for the quality in my opinion. Super comfy and love the color! Sounds great and noise cancellation works great, especially on flights. Great value for the price!!! Firstly, the quality is very good but probably not 50 dollars good, but these regularly go on sale for 25 dollars, and they are very good for that price! The battery life is goated just like all JBL products, charge em for 5 minutes and you got a few hours worth of battery! Sound quality is pretty good for what they are. I've used these for a few years now and they still hold up very nice. The range is also nice, able to stretch to 30-40 feet away.
product_name: JBL Tune 510BT - Bluetooth headphones with up to 40 hours battery, microphone for call, foldable and comfortable, Android and iOs compatible (Rose)
